title: Negative Behavior
description: Negative behaviors that impede learning or cause difficulty for those working with the child, such as crying, tantrums, property destruction, or aggression, often functioning to gain attention or escape demands.
scoring_criteria: 0 score: typically does not demonstrate behavior problems that impede learning or cause difficulty. 1 score: has some minor behavior problems (e.g., occasional crying or whining), but they are brief and recovery is quick. 2 score: emits a variety of minor negative behaviors every day (e.g., crying, verbal refusal, falling to the floor) which may be persistent and long lasting. 3 score: emits more severe and frequent negative behavior (e.g., tantrums, throwing things, property destruction) that may result in the child controlling daily activities. 4 score: negative behaviors occur several times a day and present a danger to himself or others (e.g., aggression, property destruction, or self-injurious behavior).

title: Poor Instructional Control (escape and avoidance of demands placed upon a child)
description: Behaviors emitted by a child to avoid or terminate demands, instructions, or undesirable activities. These range from mild noncompliance (looking away) to severe aggression or self-injury.
scoring_criteria: 0 score: typically cooperative with adult instructions and demands. 1 score: some demands evoke minor noncompliant behavior, but usually the child is cooperative and recovers quickly. 2 score: emits noncompliant behavior a few times a day with minor tantrums or other behaviors, and it is often difficult to get him to stop. 3 score: showing a progression towards more persistent and severe instructional control problems as measured by an increase in frequency, wider range of situations, and increased severity of avoidance/escape behavior. 4 score: noncompliant behavior is a major issue everyday and the child is quick to emit strong and severe behaviors, such as aggression or property destruction if demands are not removed.

title: Absent, Weak, or Defective Mand Repertoire
description: Difficulty acquiring an effective mand repertoire to inform adults of motivators (e.g., hunger, thirst) or remove aversive events, resulting in mands trailing behind other language skills, being prompt-bound, rote, or entirely absent.
scoring_criteria: 0 score: has an appropriate mand repertoire that is proportionate with his other skills on the Milestones Assessment. 1 score: does mand, but his LD and tact VB-MAPP Milestones Assessment scores are higher than his mand scores. 2 score: mands are few in number and limited to a small set of consumable or tangible reinforcers, despite strong tacts, LDs, and echoic skills. 3 score: mands are prompt bound, rote, scrolling occurs, responses do not match the MOs, negative behaviors function as mands, excessive or inappropriate mands occur, and the child rarely mands spontaneously. 4 score: does not have any functional mands, or demonstrates the problems listed in score 3 but does not have many other verbal skills either.

title: Absent, Weak, or Defective Tact Repertoire
description: Problems related to establishing a functional tact repertoire, such as the wrong nonverbal stimulus acquiring control (rote tacting errors), scrolling, failing to generalize, or lack of spontaneity.
scoring_criteria: 0 score: has an appropriate tact repertoire that is in proportion with his other skills on the Milestones Assessment. 1 score: can tact some items and has good echoic skills, but his listener discrimination (LD) vocabulary markedly outnumbers his tact vocabulary. 2 score: tacting errors occur frequently, the child scrolls, or the tacts are prompt bound and require frequent maintenance trials. 3 score: tact repertoire is significantly out of balance with other skills, and tacts are clearly defective as indicated by failing to generalize, lack of spontaneity, limited functional use, rote tacts, escape/avoidance during training, or failing to move beyond single word tacts. 4 score: tact repertoire is nonexistent or limited to a few tacts, despite a strong echoic and LD repertoire.

title: Absent, weak, or defective motor imitation
description: Motor imitation skills are absent, weak, or defective, potentially due to physical limitations or other barriers.
scoring_criteria: 0 score: No significant barriers in the targeted area, and no intervention is necessary. 1 score: An occasional problem exists and it should be monitored, but it may not warrant a formal intervention. 2 score: A moderate problem exists and a further analysis and possibly an intervention program should be considered before the problem becomes worse. 3 score: A persistent problem exists and is in need of a behavioral analysis and formal intervention program. 4 score: A severe problem exists and is also in need of an analysis and formal intervention program.

title: Absent, Weak, or Defective Echoic Repertoire
description: A child's ability to echo words on command is a key component of early language development. Barriers include an inability to echo words, becoming prompt bound, or exhibiting echolalia, delayed echolalia (palilalia), and excessive scripting.
scoring_criteria: 0 score: Echoic skills are consistently growing, age appropriate, and in proportion with other milestones. 1 score: Does echo, but echoic scores are lower than other milestones. 2 score: Becomes echoically prompt bound, transfer of control is difficult, or echoic acquisition is falling behind other skills. 3 score: Echoic repertoire becomes too strong (echolalia/delayed echolalia) or is relatively weak, slow to acquire, and lacks spontaneous echoic behavior. 4 score: Fails to acquire any echoic behavior despite other basic skills, or attempts to teach evoke negative behaviors.

title: Absent, Weak, or Defective Visual Perceptual Skills and Matching-to-Sample (VP-MTS)
description: The ability to attend to and discriminate among visual stimuli. Common barriers include failing to attend to the sample stimulus or scan the array, guessing, scrolling, side or position biases, and rote patterns of responding.
scoring_criteria: 0 score: VP-MTS skills are growing consistently, age appropriate, and in proportion with other milestones. 1 score: Demonstrates some matching, but VP-MTS scores are lower than other skills (especially Listener Discriminations). 2 score: Shows missing task elements (failing to scan/attend), side or position bias, scrolling, rote patterns, or is limited to small arrays. 3 score: Acquired some MTS skills but is not progressing, exhibits negative behaviors during teaching, or fails to generalize. 4 score: Fails to acquire significant MTS skills despite repeated attempts, or acquired skills are rote, defective, or never functional in the natural environment.

title: Absent, Weak, or Defective Listener Repertoires (LD and LRFFC)
description: Involves attending to a speaker, reinforcing a speaker, and demonstrating understanding of what is said (receptive language). Barriers include failing to scan arrays, position biases, scrolling, and difficulty with multiple verbal stimuli or conditional discriminations.
scoring_criteria: 0 score: Listener skills are growing consistently, age appropriate, and in proportion with other milestones. 1 score: Demonstrates some listener skills, but scores are lower than other skills (especially tacts). 2 score: Shows missing elements of the task (failing to scan/attend, early responding), side or position bias, rote patterns, scrolling, or limitations to small arrays. 3 score: Acquired some LD skills but is not progressing, exhibits negative behaviors, or struggles with complex/similar stimuli and generalization. 4 score: Fails to acquire any significant LD skills despite having other related skills.

title: Absent, weak, or defective intraverbal
description: Intraverbal skills are absent, weak, or defective, such as relying heavily on rote intraverbals.
scoring_criteria: 0 score: No significant barriers in the targeted area, and no intervention is necessary. 1 score: An occasional problem exists and it should be monitored, but it may not warrant a formal intervention. 2 score: A moderate problem exists and a further analysis and possibly an intervention program should be considered before the problem becomes worse. 3 score: A persistent problem exists and is in need of a behavioral analysis and formal intervention program. 4 score: A severe problem exists and is also in need of an analysis and formal intervention program.

title: Absent, Weak, or Defective Social Skills
description: Struggles with learning appropriate social behavior and suffers from a variety of social deficits, often due to weak motivators, aversive motivators, lack of verbal behavior, or negative behavior problems.
scoring_criteria: 0 score: No barrier (implied). 1 score: Social skills are growing consistently and are in proportion with other skills, or social behavior occurs but scores are falling behind other Milestones. 2 score: Rarely initiates a social interaction with a peer, emits socially negative behavior, or rarely spontaneously imitates, plays with, or mands to peers. 3 score: Stays by himself during play periods, does not take turns or share items, does not respond to peer's mands, or does not verbally engage with peers. 4 score: Has language skills but actively avoids other children, emits negative or inappropriate behaviors when forced into a social situation, or almost never responds to mands from peers.

title: Prompt dependent
description: A fundamental learning barrier where the child relies excessively on prompts (e.g., to emit mands) rather than independent responding.
scoring_criteria: 0 score: No significant barriers in the targeted area, and no intervention is necessary. 1 score: An occasional problem exists and it should be monitored, but it may not warrant a formal intervention. 2 score: A moderate problem exists and a further analysis and possibly an intervention program should be considered before the problem becomes worse. 3 score: A persistent problem exists and is in need of a behavioral analysis and formal intervention program. 4 score: A severe problem exists and is also in need of an analysis and formal intervention program.

title: Scrolling responses
description: The child guesses or scrolls through words when learning new vocabulary or attempting to mand.
scoring_criteria: 0 score: No significant barriers in the targeted area, and no intervention is necessary. 1 score: An occasional problem exists and it should be monitored, but it may not warrant a formal intervention. 2 score: A moderate problem exists and a further analysis and possibly an intervention program should be considered before the problem becomes worse. 3 score: A persistent problem exists and is in need of a behavioral analysis and formal intervention program. 4 score: A severe problem exists and is also in need of an analysis and formal intervention program.

title: Defective scanning skills
description: Scanning skills are defective, preventing the child from effectively locating targets in learning tasks.
scoring_criteria: 0 score: No significant barriers in the targeted area, and no intervention is necessary. 1 score: An occasional problem exists and it should be monitored, but it may not warrant a formal intervention. 2 score: A moderate problem exists and a further analysis and possibly an intervention program should be considered before the problem becomes worse. 3 score: A persistent problem exists and is in need of a behavioral analysis and formal intervention program. 4 score: A severe problem exists and is also in need of an analysis and formal intervention program.

title: A Failure to Make Conditional Discriminations
description: The child fails to learn responses involving conditional discriminations, where one stimulus changes the significance of another stimulus across multiple interlocking contingencies.
scoring_criteria: 0 score: makes conditional discriminations at a level in balance with other Milestone scores. 1 score: has trouble when the conditional discrimination requires more effort or attending (e.g., bigger arrays, similar stimuli, natural settings). 2 score: has difficulty when the stimulus involves multiple verbal components, but can respond to each independently. 3 score: fails at most tasks that involve conditional discriminations, except matching-to-sample, and frequently emits negative behavior. 4 score: does not make conditional discriminations, despite having simple discrimination skills.

title: Failure to generalize
description: A fundamental learning barrier characterized by the inability to generalize skills across time, settings, behaviors, materials, and people.
scoring_criteria: 0 score: No significant barriers in the targeted area, and no intervention is necessary. 1 score: An occasional problem exists and it should be monitored, but it may not warrant a formal intervention. 2 score: A moderate problem exists and a further analysis and possibly an intervention program should be considered before the problem becomes worse. 3 score: A persistent problem exists and is in need of a behavioral analysis and formal intervention program. 4 score: A severe problem exists and is also in need of an analysis and formal intervention program.

title: Motivation
description: The child has a limited range of motivators, both unlearned and learned, or relies on odd and aberrant motivators (e.g., self-stims, repetitive motions) that make creating and capturing motivation for teaching purposes difficult.
scoring_criteria: 0 score: Demonstrates a wide range of age-appropriate MOs, and they are predictably strong and show variation. 1 score: Motivators are slightly different from those of typically developing children. 2 score: Demonstrates MOs for odd behavior patterns or has weak MOs for age-appropriate and social reinforcers. 3 score: Demonstrates aberrant MOs for unlearned reinforcers or MOs quickly weaken after a short contact with a desired item; high rates of stereotypy. 4 score: Very limited range of MOs (perhaps just 2 or 3) that may be odd but very strong, and negative behavior occurs if not allowed to engage in them.

title: Response requirement weakens motivation
description: The effort or requirement to emit a response reduces or weakens the child's motivation to perform.
scoring_criteria: 0 score: No significant barriers in the targeted area, and no intervention is necessary. 1 score: An occasional problem exists and it should be monitored, but it may not warrant a formal intervention. 2 score: A moderate problem exists and a further analysis and possibly an intervention program should be considered before the problem becomes worse. 3 score: A persistent problem exists and is in need of a behavioral analysis and formal intervention program. 4 score: A severe problem exists and is also in need of an analysis and formal intervention program.

title: Reinforcement dependent
description: The child relies excessively on reinforcement to perform skills, indicating a dependence that impedes natural learning.
scoring_criteria: 0 score: No significant barriers in the targeted area, and no intervention is necessary. 1 score: An occasional problem exists and it should be monitored, but it may not warrant a formal intervention. 2 score: A moderate problem exists and a further analysis and possibly an intervention program should be considered before the problem becomes worse. 3 score: A persistent problem exists and is in need of a behavioral analysis and formal intervention program. 4 score: A severe problem exists and is also in need of an analysis and formal intervention program.

title: Self-stimulation
description: A specific behavior that competes with learning, characterized by self-stimulatory actions.
scoring_criteria: 0 score: No significant barriers in the targeted area, and no intervention is necessary. 1 score: An occasional problem exists and it should be monitored, but it may not warrant a formal intervention. 2 score: A moderate problem exists and a further analysis and possibly an intervention program should be considered before the problem becomes worse. 3 score: A persistent problem exists and is in need of a behavioral analysis and formal intervention program. 4 score: A severe problem exists and is also in need of an analysis and formal intervention program.

title: Articulation problems
description: Physical barriers related to articulation errors that must be overcome or accounted for during language acquisition.
scoring_criteria: 0 score: No significant barriers in the targeted area, and no intervention is necessary. 1 score: An occasional problem exists and it should be monitored, but it may not warrant a formal intervention. 2 score: A moderate problem exists and a further analysis and possibly an intervention program should be considered before the problem becomes worse. 3 score: A persistent problem exists and is in need of a behavioral analysis and formal intervention program. 4 score: A severe problem exists and is also in need of an analysis and formal intervention program.

title: Obsessive-compulsive behavior
description: Obsessive-compulsive actions or routines that compete with and impede the teaching and learning process.
scoring_criteria: 0 score: No significant barriers in the targeted area, and no intervention is necessary. 1 score: An occasional problem exists and it should be monitored, but it may not warrant a formal intervention. 2 score: A moderate problem exists and a further analysis and possibly an intervention program should be considered before the problem becomes worse. 3 score: A persistent problem exists and is in need of a behavioral analysis and formal intervention program. 4 score: A severe problem exists and is also in need of an analysis and formal intervention program.

title: Hyperactivity
description: Specific hyperactive behavior that acts as a physical or attention barrier competing with successful learning.
scoring_criteria: 0 score: No significant barriers in the targeted area, and no intervention is necessary. 1 score: An occasional problem exists and it should be monitored, but it may not warrant a formal intervention. 2 score: A moderate problem exists and a further analysis and possibly an intervention program should be considered before the problem becomes worse. 3 score: A persistent problem exists and is in need of a behavioral analysis and formal intervention program. 4 score: A severe problem exists and is also in need of an analysis and formal intervention program.

title: Failure to make eye contact, or attend to people
description: The child fails to make eye contact or attend to others, forming a social barrier to learning and language acquisition.
scoring_criteria: 0 score: No significant barriers in the targeted area, and no intervention is necessary. 1 score: An occasional problem exists and it should be monitored, but it may not warrant a formal intervention. 2 score: A moderate problem exists and a further analysis and possibly an intervention program should be considered before the problem becomes worse. 3 score: A persistent problem exists and is in need of a behavioral analysis and formal intervention program. 4 score: A severe problem exists and is also in need of an analysis and formal intervention program.

title: Sensory defensiveness
description: Behaviors stemming from sensory defensiveness that compete with the child's ability to engage and learn.
scoring_criteria: 0 score: No significant barriers in the targeted area, and no intervention is necessary. 1 score: An occasional problem exists and it should be monitored, but it may not warrant a formal intervention. 2 score: A moderate problem exists and a further analysis and possibly an intervention program should be considered before the problem becomes worse. 3 score: A persistent problem exists and is in need of a behavioral analysis and formal intervention program. 4 score: A severe problem exists and is also in need of an analysis and formal intervention program.
